name: Full in vivo OSKM reprogramming produced teratomas and iPS-like cells
summary: Abad 2013 is a safety and taxonomy boundary: in vivo reprogramming can overshoot into pluripotent or totipotency-like states with teratoma formation, so partial-reprogramming claims must stay tied to controlled, lineage-preserving schedules.
statement: Transitory in vivo OSKM induction produced teratomas in multiple organs, dedifferentiated NANOG-positive cells, reprogrammable hematopoietic cells, and circulating iPS-like cells, demonstrating the safety hazard of uncontrolled or full in vivo reprogramming.
population_or_model: Reprogrammable mice with in vivo OSKM induction
time_horizon: In vivo OSKM induction with tumor and reprogrammed-cell characterization
quantitative_note: The abstract reports teratomas emerging from multiple organs but does not provide a single incidence figure.
caveats: This is full or excessive in vivo reprogramming, not a controlled partial-reprogramming therapeutic regimen.
It is most useful as a safety and taxonomy boundary.
The result should limit overgeneralized rejuvenation claims rather than negate all partial-reprogramming approaches.
